votes.title: Bipartisan Border Security Act
votes.summary: Bipartisan bill to add 1,500 border agents, tighten asylum rules, and reform immigration processing. Hawley voted NO despite frequent public statements demanding border security.
votes.contradicts: Hawley regularly tweets about the need for border security but voted against the most significant bipartisan border security legislation in decades.
votes.moImpact: Missouri shares a border with 8 states and is a major transit corridor. Blocking border security reform affects communities along I-44 and I-70.

votes.title: Preventing Elected Leaders from Owning Securities and Investments Act
votes.summary: Would ban members of Congress and their spouses from owning or trading individual stocks.
votes.consistentNote: Hawley has consistently pushed this legislation and spoken publicly in support.

votes.title: Credit Card Rate Cap Act (Sanders-Hawley)
votes.summary: Bipartisan bill with Bernie Sanders to cap credit card interest rates at 10%. Never reached a floor vote.
votes.contradicts: Hawley later praised Trump for proposing the same policy while attacking 'The Left' — the same people who supported his bill.
votes.moImpact: Missouri households carry an average of $7,930 in credit card debt. A 10% rate cap could save the typical family hundreds of dollars per year in interest.

votes.title: American Innovation and Choice Online Act
votes.summary: Bipartisan antitrust legislation targeting dominant tech platforms.
votes.consistentNote: Hawley has consistently supported Big Tech antitrust measures and voted accordingly.

votes.title: No TikTok on Government Devices Act
votes.summary: Banned TikTok from government devices. Part of broader push to restrict Chinese tech.
votes.consistentNote: Hawley has been one of the most vocal senators on TikTok restrictions and has voted consistently.

votes.title: Railway Safety Act
votes.summary: Bipartisan railway safety bill following East Palestine derailment.
votes.consistentNote: Hawley co-sponsored and publicly advocated for this legislation consistently.
votes.moImpact: Missouri has the 6th-most rail miles in the nation. Thousands of freight trains carrying hazardous materials pass through Missouri communities every year.

votes.title: Fiscal Responsibility Act (Debt Ceiling)
votes.summary: Bipartisan deal to raise the debt ceiling and avoid default. Hawley voted NO.
votes.contradicts: Hawley has called for fiscal responsibility while voting against the debt ceiling compromise, risking default.
votes.moImpact: Missouri's agricultural sector relies heavily on credit markets. A federal default would have spiked interest rates, threatening farms and agribusinesses statewide.

votes.title: Constitutional Amendment to Overturn Citizens United
votes.summary: Hawley introduced a constitutional amendment to overturn Citizens United.
votes.contradicts: Hawley benefited from $21.2M in spending from the Senate Leadership Fund (McConnell-aligned Super PAC) in 2018 — exactly the kind of spending Citizens United enabled.
votes.moImpact: $26.6M in outside money flooded Missouri's 2018 Senate race — more than the candidates raised themselves. Missouri voters' voices were drowned out by national dark money groups.
